verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically a cytosolic chaperone involved in protein folding and stress response. However, it is a well-established example of a nominally intracellular protein with documented stress-induced and cancer-cell-restricted surface display. Multiple studies show HSPA1A translocates to the outer leaflet under heat shock, oxidative stress, hypoxia, and in tumor cells (cancer ecto-chaperone), where it becomes a target for surface-directed therapeutics (antibodies, CAR-T). This cell-state-induced ecto-pool qualifies as contextual surface accessibility despite baseline cytosolic/organellar localization.
